---
title: "Modeling Water Quality Between Upstream Sources and Municipal Drinking Water"
project-title: "Modeling Water Quality Between Upstream Sources and Municipal Drinking Water"
year: "2024"
partner: "Kansas Department of Health and Environment"
datacategory:
  - "Natural Science"
keyword:
  - "Water Quality"
datatype: "Tabular Data"
---

```{=html}

<link rel="stylesheet" href="../../project_description_style.css">

<div class="container">
    <!-- The header is now simpler as Quarto handles the title -->
    <header class="project-header">
        <p class="tagline">Project Description</p>
        <h2>{{< meta project-title >}}</h2>
        <p class="subtitle">
            A partnership with <a href="https://www.kdhe.ks.gov/">{{< meta partner >}}</a> to understand and address a key community challenge.
        </p>
    </header>

    <!-- Main Grid Container -->
    <div class="main-grid">
    
        <!-- Project Details & Resources Section -->
        <div class="top-grid">
            <!-- Project Details Card (Content is now from YAML) -->
            <div class="card">
                <h3 class="card-title">Project Details</h3>
                <ul class="details-list">
                    <li>
                        <i class="bi bi-people-fill icon"></i>
                        <div>
                            <strong>Community Partner:</strong>
                            {{< meta partner >}}
                        </div>
                    </li>
                    <li>
                       <i class="bi bi-tags-fill icon"></i>
                        <div>
                            <strong>Data Categories:</strong>
                            {{< meta datacategory >}}
                            <strong>Keywords:</strong>
                            {{< meta keyword >}} 
                            <strong>Data Type:</strong>
                            {{< meta datatype >}} 
                        </div>
                    </li>
                    <li>
                        <i class="bi bi-calendar-event-fill icon"></i>
                        <div>
                            <strong>Project Year:</strong>
                            {{< meta year >}}
                        </div>
                    </li>
                </ul>
            </div>
            <!-- Project Resources Card (Update links as needed) -->
            <div class="card">
                <h3 class="card-title">Project Resources</h3>
                <div class="resource-buttons">
                    <a href="../datasets/data_KDHE.html" class="btn btn-primary">
                        <i class="bi bi-clipboard-data-fill icon"></i> Explore the Dataset
                    </a>
                    <a href="../classplan/class_KDHE.html" class="btn btn-secondary">
                       <i class="bi bi-book-half icon"></i> View the Class Plan
                    </a>
                    <a href="CDL_Proposal_KDHE.pdf" class="btn btn-tertiary">
                        <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf-fill icon"></i> Original Proposal (PDF)
                    </a>
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
```

:::::: card
## <i class="bi bi-question-circle-fill icon"></i> The Challenge

::: conetent-text
This project seeks an economic model to establish the cost municipalities face in cleaning their water. Municipal water sources come from surface water and from groundwater. They must treat the drinking water in order to ensure that water’s safety for human consumption. Given that the sources of water for different municipalities may come with varying levels of characteristics in the water, municipalities face different costs to ensure safety. To better understand these varying costs, it is important to model how levels of characteristics in water sources relate to levels of those same characteristics that appear on the CCRs for municipal drinking water.
:::

:::: content-section
## <i class="bi bi-bar-chart-line-fill icon"></i> Key Findings & Results

::: results-placeholder
<iframe src width="100%" height="600px" style="border:none;">

</iframe>
:::
::::
::::::
